title: Tiny Habits: The Small Changes That Change Everything
author: BJ Fogg
excerpt: BJ Fogg's behavior design approach reveals that the secret to lasting change is not motivation or willpower but making behaviors so tiny they require no effort—and celebrating immediately after to rewire your brain.
keyIdeas: Behavior = Motivation x Ability x Prompt (B=MAP) — all three must align for a behavior to occur
Tiny behaviors require very little motivation; start so small it feels ridiculous
Celebration rewires your brain to associate the new habit with positive emotion
Ability is the most leverageable element — make the behavior easier, not yourself stronger
keyTakeaways: Start with habits so tiny they take less than 30 seconds — floss one tooth, do one pushup
Emotion creates habit faster than repetition — celebration after each tiny behavior is essential
Instead of trying to increase motivation, reduce the friction required to perform the behavior
Prompt design is the most overlooked element of habit formation — anchor new habits to existing routines
whoShouldRead: People who have struggled to start any habit
Anyone interested in the science behind behavior design
Readers who find Atomic Habits too complex
whoShouldSkip: Those looking for a comprehensive life system
Skeptics of positive psychology and celebration techniques
People who prefer big-picture thinking over granular tactics
